title: scleranthus annuus var. aetnensis
intro: Scleranthus annuus var. aetnensis, also known as Aetna knotgrass, is a perennial herb belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Aetna knotgrass include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and skin conditions. Aetna knotgrass is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in poultices and salves to topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
